behaviors.description: Reviewer dispatch sends the same source document and correct reviewer-specific instruction pack to each reviewer role.
behaviors.justification: Escalation quote: "sends the same source document and correct reviewer-specific instruction pack to each reviewer role." This is a module-handoff correctness risk (dispatcher -> per-reviewer invoker), not single-function-only logic. REAL-INFRA is not required: in-process reviewer adapters can catch payload/routing mismatches. Rejected higher levels: no behavior text requires real OS/network/subprocess, and nothing in this behavior depends on model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire dispatcher with three in-process fake reviewer clients; execute one dispatch; assert each fake receives the identical document bytes/id plus the correct role-specific instruction pack, with no cross-role mix-up.

behaviors.description: The live clause extractor identifies key terms (parties, dates, obligations, penalties) present in the contract.
behaviors.justification: Escalation quote: "live clause extractor identifies key terms ... present in the contract." Correctness depends on real model extraction quality; a stub would hide failures in semantic detection/recall. Rejected Workflow: behavior names one reviewer’s output quality, not a multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real clause-extractor model on a curated contract set with gold key-term annotations; assert required term classes are extracted within agreed quality thresholds (for example recall/precision minima).

behaviors.description: Risk-policy configuration is passed correctly to the risk assessor invocation.
behaviors.justification: Escalation quote: "configuration is passed correctly to the risk assessor invocation." This is invocation wiring/state handoff across modules. Rejected Agentic by behavior text "passed correctly ... invocation" (transport/config correctness, not model judgment quality). Rejected System: no requirement for real network/subprocess/OS effects.
behaviors.test_description: Construct orchestrator + risk-invoker with an in-process capture stub; invoke with a policy fixture; assert serialized request to risk assessor contains exact policy fields/version/overrides expected.

behaviors.description: The live risk assessor flags language that is problematic under policy, including indirect or euphemistic phrasing.
behaviors.justification: Escalation quote: "live risk assessor flags language ... including indirect or euphemistic phrasing." Detecting indirect/euphemistic risk is model semantic judgment; deterministic mocks would mask true failures.
behaviors.test_description: Evaluate the real risk-assessor model on contracts/snippets containing direct and euphemistic risky language labeled by policy experts; assert detection quality targets and acceptable false-negative rate for euphemistic cases.

behaviors.description: Jurisdiction and regulatory profile are passed correctly to the compliance checker invocation.
behaviors.justification: Escalation quote: "passed correctly to the compliance checker invocation." Failure boundary is module handoff (orchestrator/config -> compliance call payload). Rejected Agentic: behavior is about parameter passing, not compliance decision quality. Rejected System: can be caught with in-process invocation capture.
behaviors.test_description: Run orchestrator/compliance-invoker with a capture stub; provide jurisdiction + regulatory profile matrix; assert each invocation payload carries exact jurisdiction code/profile ids and no leakage from other runs.

behaviors.description: The live compliance checker correctly determines whether the contract meets all configured regulatory requirements.
behaviors.justification: Escalation quote: "live compliance checker correctly determines whether the contract meets all configured regulatory requirements." This is model decision correctness; stubs cannot validate real determination quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real compliance-checker model on jurisdiction-specific contracts with expert pass/fail labels and requirement-level rationales; assert decision accuracy and requirement-coverage thresholds.

behaviors.description: When two reviewers flag the same clause with conflicting assessments, the merge step preserves both assessments with attribution.
behaviors.justification: Escalation quote checked: "merge step preserves both assessments with attribution." This can be validated at pure merge-function/object boundary with deterministic inputs; no I/O, no model call required. Rejected Integration/System/Agentic/Workflow because behavior text does not require external infrastructure or live model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Call merge function with fixture outputs containing same clause id and conflicting labels (for example `standard` vs `high risk`); assert unified report includes both assessments and correct reviewer attribution fields.

behaviors.description: End-to-end orchestration (dispatch all three reviewers, collect outputs, merge unified report) succeeds on a real contract without losing any reviewer findings.
behaviors.justification: Escalation quote: "dispatch all three reviewers, collect outputs, merge unified report" plus "real contract" and "without losing any reviewer findings." This is multiple agentic steps in sequence/parallel with aggregation; correctness depends on cross-step handoff of real reviewer outputs. Rejected lower levels: Integration/Agentic alone cannot expose full pipeline loss/regression across all stages.
behaviors.test_description: Execute full pipeline on real contracts through live extractor, live risk assessor, and live compliance checker; then merge. Assert every reviewer finding appears in final unified report with attribution and no dropped/overwritten items.
